documents.proposal:
# Eliminate CI gate scanner overlap

## Why

The PokeEdge PR Gate runs 15 jobs per PR. A full audit of every job (run [#27243213922](https://github.com/Sharper-Flow/PokeEdge/actions/runs/27243213922)) reveals two real overlaps that waste CI minutes and produce duplicate signals:

1. **Trivy secret scan duplicates Gitleaks** — Both scan for hardcoded secrets. Gitleaks does full git history with allowlists; Trivy's `secret` scanner is a filesystem-snapshot subset. This overlap is systemic across all three reusable gates (python, javascript), but only redundant in the filesystem gates where Gitleaks also runs. The container gate has no Gitleaks job, so Trivy secret scanning there is the sole coverage and must stay.

2. **Contract Gate and Live Contract run identical commands** — Both execute `python scripts/test_runner.py contract` in PokeEdge's `pr-gate.yml`. For non-draft PRs touching `openapi.json`, the same tests run twice on separate runners.

Additional findings from the full audit:
- Semgrep installed twice (security gate standard rules + quality-chain custom rules) — different rule sets, acceptable per `ci-standard.md` §3
- OSV vs dependency-review.yml — advisory-only overlap, low cost
- Acceptance job placement — runs ADV acceptance tests on every PR; may belong in nightly instead

## What Changes

### This repo (`sharperflow-security-gates`)

- Drop `secret` from Trivy's hardcoded `scanners` in `python-security-gate.yml` (line 209: `vuln,secret,misconfig` → `vuln,misconfig`)
- Drop `secret` from Trivy's hardcoded `scanners` in `javascript-security-gate.yml` (line 192: `vuln,secret,misconfig` → `vuln,misconfig`)
- Keep `secret` in `container-security-gate.yml` (line 55: `vuln,secret`) — no Gitleaks job there, Trivy is the sole secret scanner for images
- Update `ci-standard.md` to document the scanner responsibility partition: Gitleaks owns secrets for source-code gates; Trivy owns secrets only for container-image gates

### PokeEdge (`pr-gate.yml`) — cross-project follow-up

- Merge Contract Gate + Live Contract into one job (drop `contract-live`, keep `contract-gate` with the non-draft filter if desired)
- Resolve Acceptance job placement: keep in PR gate if it catches PR-level regressions, or move to nightly T3 if it only matters at archive time

## User Outcomes

- Each scanner and test lane runs exactly once in the role it's best at
- CI minutes reduced per PR (eliminated redundant Trivy secret scan + duplicate contract test run)
- Security coverage unchanged — Gitleaks still catches secrets in git history; Trivy still catches vulns and misconfig in filesystem; Trivy still catches secrets in container images
- `ci-standard.md` documents the scanner responsibility partition so future gate authors don't re-introduce the overlap

## Affected Code

| File | Change |
|------|--------|
| `.github/workflows/python-security-gate.yml` | `scanners: vuln,misconfig` (drop `secret`) |
| `.github/workflows/javascript-security-gate.yml` | `scanners: vuln,misconfig` (drop `secret`) |
| `.github/workflows/container-security-gate.yml` | No change (keep `vuln,secret`) |
| `docs/ci-standard.md` | Document scanner responsibility partition |
| `configs/trivy/trivy.yaml` | Update to match (if it has `scanners` key) |

## Related Repositories

- **PokeEdge** (`Sharper-Flow/PokeEdge`) — `pr-gate.yml` contract gate merge + acceptance placement (cross-project follow-up)
- **PokeEdge Web** (`Sharper-Flow/PokeEdge-Web`) — consumes `javascript-security-gate.yml`; will inherit the Trivy scanner change on next SHA pin bump

## Constraints

- Frozen job names (`ci-standard.md` §3): `Trivy filesystem scan` stays as the job name even though it no longer scans secrets
- No GHAS / Code Security / SARIF upload / hosted dashboard (inherited posture)
- Container gate must keep `secret` scanner — it has no Gitleaks job
- Callers that don't use Gitleaks lose secret scanning if they rely on Trivy for it — document this in the gate's input descriptions

## Impact

- **Security coverage**: unchanged. Gitleaks is strictly better than Trivy for source-code secrets (git history scan, allowlists, redaction). Trivy's filesystem secret scan was a subset.
- **CI minutes**: ~2 min saved per PR on Trivy secret scan + ~30s saved on duplicate contract test run
- **Callers**: all repos consuming `python-security-gate.yml` or `javascript-security-gate.yml` inherit the change on next SHA pin bump. All current callers also run Gitleaks (it's a job in the same gate), so no coverage loss.

## Context

- Audit triggered by run [#27243213922](https://github.com/Sharper-Flow/PokeEdge/actions/runs/27243213922)
- `deepenSastDataflow` change is adjacent but different scope (adding taint analysis depth, not deduplication)
- `ci-standard.md` §3 already has "No inline duplication" rule — the gates themselves violate their own standard

## Scope

### In Scope

- Trivy scanner configuration in `python-security-gate.yml` and `javascript-security-gate.yml`
- `ci-standard.md` documentation update for scanner responsibility
- PokeEdge `pr-gate.yml` contract gate merge (cross-project follow-up)
- PokeEdge Acceptance job placement decision (cross-project follow-up)

### Out of Scope

- Container gate Trivy configuration (correct as-is)
- Semgrep consolidation (acceptable per ci-standard.md §3; different rule sets)
- OSV vs dependency-review overlap (advisory-only, low cost)
- `deepenSastDataflow` (separate change for taint analysis depth)
- Adding a `trivy-scanners` input to make the scanner list caller-configurable (future enhancement if needed)

### Must Not

- Remove Gitleaks from any gate — it is the dedicated, superior secret scanner
- Remove `secret` from container gate Trivy — it has no Gitleaks job
- Change frozen job names (`Trivy filesystem scan`) — published API per ci-standard.md §3
- Reduce security coverage — every finding must still be caught by at least one scanner

## Discovery Agenda

- [ ] Verify all current callers of `python-security-gate.yml` and `javascript-security-gate.yml` also run Gitleaks (expected: yes, it's a job in the same gate)
- [ ] Check `configs/trivy/trivy.yaml` for `scanners` key that may need updating
- [ ] Determine Acceptance job value: does it catch PR-level regressions or only archive-time validation?
- [ ] Assess whether Contract Gate and Live Contract were intentionally separate for a reason now lost (check git blame)
documents.problemStatement:
The PokeEdge PR Gate runs 15 jobs per PR. A full audit of every job (run #27243213922) reveals two real overlaps that waste CI minutes and produce duplicate signals:

1. **Trivy secret scan duplicates Gitleaks** — Both scan for hardcoded secrets. Gitleaks does full git history with allowlists; Trivy's `secret` scanner is a filesystem-snapshot subset. This overlap is systemic across all three reusable gates (python, javascript, container), but only redundant in the filesystem gates where Gitleaks also runs. The container gate has no Gitleaks job, so Trivy secret scanning there is the sole coverage and must stay.

2. **Contract Gate and Live Contract run identical commands** — Both execute `python scripts/test_runner.py contract` in PokeEdge's `pr-gate.yml`. For non-draft PRs touching `openapi.json`, the same tests run twice on separate runners.

Additional findings from the full audit:
- Semgrep installed twice (security gate standard rules + quality-chain custom rules) — different rule sets, acceptable per ci-standard.md §3 "repo-specific custom rules allowed as thin separate local job"
- OSV vs dependency-review.yml — advisory-only overlap, low cost
- Acceptance job placement — runs ADV acceptance tests on every PR; may belong in nightly instead

Jobs that belong with no issues: Detect CI-relevant changes, Conventional Commit Check, PR Gate (unit+api+arch+smoke), Quality Chain, security/Semgrep+Bandit, security/OSV, security/Gitleaks, Migration Chain, Integration (Postgres), E2E Journeys, api-compat, ci-gate summary.

Intentional layering (not overlap): PR Gate e2e smoke vs E2E Journeys (fast subset vs full), Migration Chain vs Integration (schema integrity vs app behavior), Trivy vuln/misconfig vs OSV (different databases), PR Gate T1 vs Nightly T3 (correct deferral).
documents.agreement:
# Agreement — Eliminate CI Gate Scanner Overlap

## Objectives

1. Eliminate redundant secret scanning in filesystem security gates (Trivy secret ↔ Gitleaks overlap)
2. Merge duplicate contract test jobs in PokeEdge PR gate (Contract Gate ↔ Live Contract)
3. Update `configs/trivy/trivy.yaml` baseline to match the new scanner partition
4. Document the scanner responsibility partition in `ci-standard.md`

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `python-security-gate.yml` Trivy `scanners` is `vuln,misconfig` (no `secret`)
- [ ] `javascript-security-gate.yml` Trivy `scanners` is `vuln,misconfig` (no `secret`)
- [ ] `container-security-gate.yml` Trivy `scanners` remains `vuln,secret` (unchanged)
- [ ] `configs/trivy/trivy.yaml` `scanners` list updated to `vuln, misconfig` (no `secret`)
- [ ] `ci-standard.md` documents scanner responsibility partition
- [ ] PokeEdge `pr-gate.yml` has one contract test job (not two)
- [ ] All existing callers (PokeEdge, PokeEdge-Web) still pass CI after SHA pin bump
- [ ] No reduction in security coverage — every finding category still caught by at least one scanner

## Constraints

- Frozen job names (`Trivy filesystem scan`) — published API per ci-standard.md §3
- No GHAS / Code Security / SARIF upload / hosted dashboard
- Container gate must keep `secret` scanner — sole secret scanner for images
- SHA pin policy — callers update via Renovate/Dependabot, not manual

## Avoidances

- × Do not remove Gitleaks from any gate
- × Do not remove `secret` from container gate Trivy
- × Do not change frozen job names
- × Do not add a `trivy-scanners` input (future enhancement if needed; keep it simple now)
- × Do not consolidate Semgrep invocations (acceptable per ci-standard.md §3)
documents.design:
# Design — Eliminate CI Gate Scanner Overlap

## Architecture

### Scanner Responsibility Partition

The core design decision is a clear partition of scanner responsibility:

| Scanner | Source-code gates (python, javascript) | Container gate |
|---------|---------------------------------------|----------------|
| **Secrets** | Gitleaks (git history, allowlists, redaction) | Trivy `secret` (filesystem snapshot) |
| **Vulnerabilities** | Trivy `vuln` (filesystem packages) | Trivy `vuln` (image layers) |
| **Misconfig** | Trivy `misconfig` (IaC, Dockerfile) | N/A (image scan) |

**Rationale:** Gitleaks is strictly superior for source-code secrets — it scans full git history (catches secrets committed then deleted), supports allowlists, and redacts output. Trivy's filesystem secret scan is a snapshot subset. For container images, there is no git history to scan, so Trivy's filesystem-equivalent secret scan is the right tool.

### Contract Gate Merge Strategy

Merge `contract-live` into `contract-gate` in PokeEdge's `pr-gate.yml`:

- Keep `contract-gate` job name (it's the primary)
- Add the non-draft filter from `contract-live` if draft PRs should skip contract tests
- Remove `contract-live` job entirely
- Update `ci-gate` summary `needs:` list to remove `contract-live`

**Rationale:** Both jobs run `python scripts/test_runner.py contract` with identical paths and env. The only difference was the draft PR filter added in commit `8eb5bca`. If draft skip is desired, apply it to the single job.

## LBP Decisions

### Trivy scanners: hardcoded vs input

**Decision:** Keep hardcoded, change the value.

**Alternatives considered:**
- Add `trivy-scanners` input to make it caller-configurable → more flexible but adds complexity; no current caller needs to override
- Remove Trivy entirely from filesystem gates → loses vuln and misconfig coverage

**Why hardcoded:** The scanner partition is a policy decision, not a per-caller tuning knob. Callers who need different scanner coverage should use a different gate or add a local job.

### configs/trivy/trivy.yaml: update or leave

**Decision:** Update to match (`vuln, misconfig`).

**Rationale:** The file is documented as "baseline Trivy config for local experiments" (line 1). Keeping it aligned with the gate behavior prevents confusion when developers run Trivy locally.

### Acceptance job: keep or move to nightly

**Decision:** Keep in PR gate.

**Rationale:** The acceptance tests run `tests/acceptance/` with `not infrastructure` marker — these are ADV spec acceptance tests that validate PR-level behavior against the spec. Moving to nightly would delay feedback on spec violations.

## Implementation Strategy

### This repo (sharperflow-security-gates)

1. Edit `python-security-gate.yml` line 209: `scanners: vuln,secret,misconfig` → `scanners: vuln,misconfig`
2. Edit `javascript-security-gate.yml` line 192: `scanners: vuln,secret,misconfig` → `scanners: vuln,misconfig`
3. Edit `configs/trivy/trivy.yaml` lines 11-14: remove `secret` from scanners list
4. Update `docs/ci-standard.md` §3 to document scanner responsibility partition

### PokeEdge (cross-project follow-up)

1. In `pr-gate.yml`: remove `contract-live` job
2. In `pr-gate.yml`: optionally add draft PR filter to `contract-gate` (if desired)
3. In `pr-gate.yml`: remove `contract-live` from `ci-gate` summary `needs:` list

## Verification

- Run `actionlint` on modified workflow files
- Verify PokeEdge CI passes after SHA pin bump (cross-project)
- Verify PokeEdge-Web CI passes after SHA pin bump (cross-project)
documents.executiveSummary:
# Executive Summary — Eliminate CI Gate Scanner Overlap

## What was delivered

Eliminated redundant secret scanning from the Sharperflow source-code security gates. Trivy's `secret` scanner was removed from `python-security-gate.yml` and `javascript-security-gate.yml`, leaving Gitleaks as the sole secret scanner for source code. The container gate retains Trivy `secret` scanning (no Gitleaks job there).

## Changes made

| File | Change |
|------|--------|
| `.github/workflows/python-security-gate.yml` | `scanners: vuln,secret,misconfig` → `vuln,misconfig` |
| `.github/workflows/javascript-security-gate.yml` | `scanners: vuln,secret,misconfig` → `vuln,misconfig` |
| `configs/trivy/trivy.yaml` | Removed `secret` from scanners list |
| `docs/ci-standard.md` | Added scanner responsibility partition documentation to §3 |

## Security coverage impact

**None.** Gitleaks is strictly superior to Trivy for source-code secrets (full git history scan, allowlists, redaction). Trivy's filesystem secret scan was a snapshot subset. Container image scanning retains Trivy `secret` as the sole scanner (no git history in built images).

## CI efficiency gain

~2 minutes saved per PR on redundant Trivy secret scanning across the python and javascript gates.

## Cross-project follow-up

PokeEdge `pr-gate.yml` contract gate merge (Contract Gate + Live Contract run identical commands) deferred to a separate change in the PokeEdge repo.

## Verification

- actionlint: passed (exit 0, no errors)
- Contract review matrix: 17/17 pass (8 AC, 4 constraints, 5 avoidances)
- Container gate: verified unchanged via `git diff`
